Solution:
N2KSQ instrument running system suite v1.2 using RNA and Single-Cell pipelines and can experience GRAGEN analysis failure due to incorrect permissions. The failure results in red 'x' indicator next to the secondary analysis run in prcess management with no reported error in control software. The following detailed error message should be present in the analysis stderr.log files:

"Cannot open/usr/local/ill/genpmes/hg38_at_aware/GRAGEN/8/hash_table.bin"

This is caused when DRAGEN cannot access the reference directory.

The fix should be done during installation of new instruments until the fix has been cut into manufacturing or can be applied later by support personnel. For more information about this issue, see FAN-8784.

1. Close control software.
2. Open terminal by selecting Applications | System Tools | Terminal
3. Run commands as ilmnadmin account by entering the following command:
su ilmnadmin
4. Enter the ilmnadmin account password
5. Check permissions on the /usr/local/ill/genomes/hg38_alt_aware/DRAGEN/8
6. Change permissions on the entire genomes directory by entering the following command:
chmod -R 775 /usr/local/ill/genome
7. Confirm that the permissions are correct. Enter the dollowing command and compare the results with those in step 5:
getfacl /usr/local/ill/genomes/hg38_alt_aware/DRAGEN/8
8. Close the terminal window.

If you open the S_SW, the screen show "Required folder does not exist: /usr/local/ill/testdata".
Create testdata of directory

1. Confirm log in ilmnadmin account
2. Close NCS or S_SW, if there still open.
3. Select Applications, choose Files open file browser
4. Open user/local/ill directory
5. Create testdata and inputsheet file
* Select Terminal on the file browser
* Input the code
sudo mkdir /usr/local/ill/testdata
sudo chmod 777 /usr/local/ill/testdata
* Repeat the same action at the Iuputsheet directory
6. Transfer the parameter into /usr/loacl/ill/iuputsheet directory
7. Open S_SW and input the password
8. Confirm the path of input and output in S_SW are set.
* In the menu, and select Inutput Output Repository
* Confirm Test Configuration and Pass/Fail Directory be set the following path, if not immediately set /usr/local/ill/inputsheet
* Confirm Primary Output Repository be set the following path, if not immediately set usr/loacl/ill/testdata

Error message: The analysis specified requires GenerateFastQ 4.2.11workflow. Go to DRAGEN screen and install the workflow.
Solution:
The IDT for ill Unique Dual (UD) index adapters are arranged in the plate to enforce the recommended pairing strategy. The index adapters are 10 bases long, instead of the typical eight bases.  The IDT for ill UD Indexes include the following:  * IDT for ill DNA/RNA UD Indexes * IDT for ill PCR UD Indexes * IDT for ill Nextera DNA UD Indexes Index 1 (i7) Adapters  CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT[i7]GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG  Index 2 (i5) Adapters  AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[i5]TCGTCGGCAGCGTC  https://support-docs.ill.com/SHARE/AdapterSequences/Content/IDTill-UDIndexes.htm

Use the following instructions to store a thawed cartridge and flow cell in the event of an instrument error during the instrument pre-run check prior to the fluidics check.

1. Sperate the flow cell cartridge
2. Remove and discard diluted library from the reservoir (up to ~18 ul)
* Prepare a fresh dilution of the same library for the next run to avoid sample cross-contamination with residual library in the reservoir
3. Position the cartridge in 2℃ to 8℃ storage so that the label faces up and air can circulate on all sides.
Do not exceed 72 hours. If cartridge was thawes in the refrigerator for 12 hours overnight, do not exceed 60 hours.
4. Retrun the flow cell to the original silver foil packing with the desiccant
5. Tape the foil packing shut and place in 2℃ to 8℃ storage.
Do not exceed 72 hours

1. Select Eject Cartridge to open the visor and eject the tray
2. Select Eject Cartridge from the tray.
3. Remove the cartridge from the tray
4. Store or dispose of the cartridge, depending in when the cancellation occurred.
5. Select Close Door to reload the tray and return to the Home screen

If an error display for Status of Secondary Analysis in Process Management, you can requeue the run to perform on-instrument DRAGEN analysis again on the generated cBCL files. The original run folder must still be present on the instrument for requeue functionality. Using this requeue functionality does not requeue runs in BaseSpace Sequence Hub. To requeue in BaseSpace Sequence Hub, see Fix Sample Sheet in BaseSpace Sequence Hub Help Center.

1. Update your smaple sheet v2, and then save the sample sheet to a potable or mounted network drive.
2. If you saved the sample sheet to a portable drive, plug the drive into a USB3.0 port, located on both the side and back of the instrument. Gently move the instrument as needed to access the back.
3. Select the control software menu, and then select Process  Management
4. Make sure that there are no sequencing runs or on-instrument secondary analysis in progresss
5. Select Requeue next to the completed run to requeue
6. Select Choose to navigate to the updated sample sheet, and then select open
7. Select Start Requeue

Power cycling the instrument safely shuts down and restart the system to restore a lost connection, align a specificaiton, or resolve an initialzation failure. Software message indicate when power cycle to resolve am error or warning. 

1. From the control software menu, select Shut Down Instrument
2. If the system does not shut down, hold the power button on the right side of the instrument until the lights fade
3. When the power buttom pulse, press the power off (O) side of the toggle switch on the rear panel.
The power button might continue to pulse after the power is turned offf
4. Wait 30 seconds
5. Press the power on (I) side of the toggle switch
6. When the power button pulse, press it.
7. Wait about 5 minutes for the operating system to load. When the operating system is loaded, log on to the system
The control software is launched and initializes the system. Wait abot 5 minutes for system initailization. The Home screen appears when initialization is complete.
